$comment: Machine-readable frontmatter contract (#130 slice A, docs/data-model.md D2). To add or change a field: edit its descriptor here FIRST, then the code, then the conformance suites — mcp-server/tests/frontmatter-contract.test.ts pins the TS write sets (create_note's written frontmatter and update_note's PATCHABLE_FRONTMATTER_KEYS), cli/tests/test_frontmatter_contract.py pins ingest's read set (via a source scan of cli/schist/ingest.py) and every invalid-coercion policy behaviorally. Drift in either language fails that language's CI. writtenBy scope: 'create_note'/'update_note' are the MCP tools and are conformance-enforced; 'cli_add' documents the `schist add` CLI (cli/schist/commands.py), which writes title/date/status/tags/concepts/file_ref with NONE of the MCP normalizations or validations and is not conformance-tested. Consumers must ignore descriptor keys they do not recognize, so new keys can be added without breaking either suite.

fields.notes: Ingest fallback chain: title -> topic -> concept -> filename-derived title. Only non-empty STRING candidates are picked; non-string or empty values fall through the chain.
ISO 8601 date. Ingest str()'s any non-null value (YAML date objects become 'YYYY-MM-DD'); TS round-trips coerce Date back to the date-only string so edits never rewrite it to a full ISO timestamp.
Write side validates against the vault's configured statuses (#276); ingest only type-guards string so a non-string never lands with native SQLite affinity (#278).
Leading '#' stripped and items trimmed on both write and ingest. Non-list values yield no tags. Stored as a JSON array (also concepts.tags on concept nodes); empty -> NULL.

Slugs normalized with the shared rules pinned by schema/concept-slug-parity.json (#303/#318). On non-concept documents each slug also creates an implicit 'references' edge on ingest; concept notes only register the slugs, no edge. Stored as a JSON array; empty -> NULL.
NULL means 'not declared' and is deliberately distinct from 'medium' (#69). Write side rejects off-enum values; ingest coerces them to NULL.
Informative external-file pointer only; schist never stores, syncs, or validates the referenced file. Empty string or non-string -> NULL.

Original authoring agent stamped by create_note. Deliberately NOT patchable (provenance must not be forgeable) and NOT read by ingest — docs.source is fed by the separate 'source' field, not this one.

Deliberately not writable via MCP tools: frontmatter scope overrides the directory-derived scope, so a patchable scope would let a path-authorized caller spoof graph read-visibility. Ingest fallback chain: frontmatter -> parent directory -> 'global'.
Content-origin marker, distinct from source_agent (which names the authoring agent). Off-enum values coerce to NULL. Not writable via MCP tools (provenance must not be forgeable).
Legacy title fallback for hand-authored notes: used only when 'title' is absent, empty, or non-string.
Presence marks the note as a concept node (as does living under concepts/). Value is slug-normalized; non-string falls back to the filename stem. Also third in the title fallback chain.

Citation-order author names. A bare string is wrapped into a one-element list; non-string list items are dropped; a non-string scalar (e.g. authors: 42) coerces to NULL. Stored as a JSON array; empty -> NULL.
Publication year, not note creation date. Digit-only strings coerce to int; booleans and anything else -> NULL.
Journal, conference, publisher, archive, or dataset venue.
Local conventions: journal|preprint|conference|book|thesis|report|dataset. Not enforced by ingest — any scalar is stringified.
DOI without a URL wrapper.
Quote in YAML — bare 2004.00001 parses as a float and gets stringified.
Bare YAML integers are stringified on ingest.
Stable local BibTeX key.
Canonical publisher, DOI, archive, or project URL.

Verification record. paper_metadata.verified is 1 iff verification.verified_on is present. A non-object value is treated as absent (verified=0, all derived columns NULL). Dotted field names below denote NESTED mapping keys — a literal flat 'verification.verified_on:' frontmatter key is ignored entirely. Presence of any top-level paper field — or a papers/ path — triggers the paper_metadata row.
Verification date. Its presence flips paper_metadata.verified to 1.
Agent or human that performed the verification.
Sources in decreasing authority (crossref:<doi>, pubmed:<pmid>, ...). Mapping items are flattened to 'key:value' strings; a bare string is wrapped. Stored as a JSON array; empty -> NULL.
